LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY (ERRONEOUSLY SUED) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF DID NOT CHECK THE TENTATIVE RULING OR INFORM COURT AND COUNSEL OF HIS INTENT TO ARGUE. THE COURT HEARS ARGUMENTS ON THE MOTION AND ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE (CODE CIV. PROC., 430.10(E)), AND IS ALSO BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. PLAINTIFF FILED AN APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO PRESENT A LATE CLAIM AGAINST CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, WHICH THIS COURT DENIED ON NOVEMBER 7, 2019. (IN RE: MICHAEL L. BEAUCHAMP, CPF-19-516867.) IN THE ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION, THE COURT FOUND THAT THE ALLEGED INCIDENT IN THE APPLICATION OCCURRED ON OCTOBER 11, 1990, OVER 29 YEARS AGO. (RJN, EX. D.) PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT ALLEGES THE SAME FACTS THAT WERE ALLEGED IN PLAINTIFF'S PREVIOUS APPLICATION. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES "DEFENDANTS NEGLIGENTLY PARTICIPATED IN A COPY CAT MURDER FOR HIRE SCHEME AND ATTEMPTED TO MURDER PLAINTIFF WITH A KILLING MACHINE, CAUSING PLAINTIFF UPON LEARNING OF THE SCOPE OF THE MURDER SCHEME TO SUFFER PTSD." (COMPL., P. 1.) THE ALLEGED FACTS REFER TO THE 1990 INCIDENT. PLAINTIFF'S GENERAL NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IS UNTIMELY AND IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (CODE CIV. PROC., 335 AND 335.1; SEE SO V. SHIN (2013) 212 CAL.APP.4TH 652, 670.) COUNSEL IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. THE COURT INFORMS PLAINTIFF THAT IT EXPECTS PLAINTIFF TO CHECK THE TENTATIVE RULING AND COMPLY WITH THE RULES GOING FORWARD. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, (E: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2020 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ray Stevens, Esq. DeputyCity Attorney, T:415.554.3800, for defendant City and County of San Francisco, by Court Call;
Zheying Li, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Broodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP, T:415.421.3400, for defendant Kansai Development, by Court Call;
Enoch Wang, Esq. of Fife Law, LLP, T:415.837.3101, for defendant Bay Area Christian Church, by Court Call;
Mitchell Vanlandingham, Esq. of Wood Smith Henning & Berman, T:925.222.3400, for defendant ParkMerced Owner, LLC, by Court Call;
No appearance by self-represented plaintiff Michael Beauchamp,

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for negligence (Code Civ. Proc., 430.10(e)), Plaintiff did not comply with the Government Claims Act. Plaintiff filed an Application for Leave to Present a Late Claim Against City and County of San Francisco, which this court denied on November 7, 2019. (In Re: Michael L. Beauchamp, CPF-19-516867.) In the order denying plaintiff's application, the court found that the alleged incident in the application occurred on October 11, 1990, over 29 years ago. (RJN, Ex. D.) Plaintiff's complaint alleges the same facts that were alleged in plaintiff's previous application. The complaint alleges "Defendants negligently participated in a Copy Cat murder for hire scheme and attempted to murder Plaintiff with a Killing Machine, causing Plaintiff upon learning of the scope of the murder scheme to suffer PTSD." (Compl., p. 1.) The alleged facts refer to the 1990 incident. Plaintiff's general negligence cause of action is untimely and is barred by the statute of limitations. (Code Civ. Proc., 335 and 335.1; see So v. Shin (2013) 212 Cal.App.4th 652, 670.) Plaintiff has not shown a reasonable probability that he can amend to overcome these defects.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
